name: Meal Planner
description: Get a full week of meals with a consolidated grocery list, personalized to your family's size, dietary needs, budget, and cooking skill. Reuses ingredients across meals to cut waste and cost.
author: ProwlrBot Team

before_after.before: Standing in the kitchen at 5:30 PM with no plan, ordering takeout 3 times a week at $45 a pop, throwing away forgotten groceries every Sunday
before_after.after: A full weekly meal plan with recipes and a sorted grocery list every Sunday morning — eating better, spending $120 less per week on food

user_stories.quote: I feed a family of five and one kid is gluten-free. This plans around all of that AND keeps us under $150 a week. I didn't think that was possible.
user_stories.name: Michelle R.

user_stories.quote: I'm a terrible cook but this gives me dead-simple recipes with like 6 ingredients. I've actually cooked dinner every night this week for the first time in years.
user_stories.name: James H.

user_stories.quote: The grocery list is sorted by aisle and factors in what I already have. My weekly shop went from an hour of wandering to 25 focused minutes.
user_stories.name: Patricia D.

setup_steps.instruction: Tell the planner how many people you're cooking for and any dietary restrictions (vegetarian, gluten-free, etc.).
Set your cooking skill level and max time per meal (15, 30, or 45+ minutes).
Enter your weekly grocery budget so the planner keeps meals within range.
Optionally list what you already have in your fridge or pantry to reduce the shopping list.
Choose your planning day (Sunday, Saturday, etc.) and delivery channel for the weekly plan.
